sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Bayer 04 Leverkusen have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, with 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses, displaying attacking intent with an average of 1.2 goals per game. 1. FC Köln, on the other hand, are in a slump with no wins in their last 4 matches (2 draws, 2 losses), though they’ve managed to score in every game, averaging 1.4 goals per match. Leverkusen's superior league position (6th) and home advantage at BayArena give them an edge.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Leverkusen have had the upper hand, winning 2-0 away and drawing 2-2 at home with a dramatic late surge. Historically, Leverkusen have won 3 of the last 5 matchups against Köln, with high-scoring games (over 2.5 goals in 8 of the last 10) being a recurring theme.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Leverkusen dominate in possession (59.7%, 2nd in the league) and big chances created (47, 2nd), though they’ve underperformed their expected goals by -5.8. Köln struggle defensively, conceding in all of their last 8 games, but have a knack for late goals (52% of goals after the 76th minute). Leverkusen’s aerial strength (56.5% duels won, 1st) could exploit Köln’s vulnerabilities.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: Leverkusen’s Aleix García and Ibrahim Maza are in fine form, with high Sofascore ratings and recent contributions in goals and assists. For Köln, El Mala Said has been a standout with 3 goals in the last 5 matches, posing a threat on the counter. Jonas Hofmann’s past impact against Köln (1 goal, 4 assists in 5 games) could be decisive.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Leverkusen are likely to control possession and push for early goals, as they’ve scored first in 6 of their last 8 matches. Köln’s tendency to concede (no clean sheets in 8 games) and their reliance on late goals suggest a defensive approach with counter-attacks. Expect Leverkusen to dominate corners (average 5.8 per game) and exploit set-pieces.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Considering Leverkusen’s home advantage, superior form, and head-to-head dominance, they are favored to win, aligning with Sofascore’s 52% probability for a home victory. Köln’s scoring consistency might see them nick a goal, but their defensive frailties are likely to be exposed. A high-scoring affair is probable given historical trends and current streaks.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, the scoreline remains level at 0-0 between Bayer 04 Leverkusen and 1. FC Köln. Leverkusen have dominated possession and created more opportunities in the attacking third, showing a clear intent to dictate the pace of the game. However, their inability to convert big chances has kept the game goalless. On the other hand, Köln have been more defensive, with limited forays into the final third, but they’ve managed to hold their ground and frustrate Leverkusen’s attack.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Leverkusen's attacking pressure
halftimeSections.text: Bayer 04 Leverkusen have been the more aggressive side, consistently pushing into the opposition’s penalty area and creating multiple openings. Their dominance in duels, especially in the air, has given them an edge in maintaining control of the game. Despite missing key opportunities, their persistent pressure suggests they are the more likely team to break the deadlock. Players like Aleix García and Malik Tillman have been pivotal in creating chances, while Christian Kofane showed threat in front of goal.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Köln's defensive resilience
halftimeSections.text: 1. FC Köln have adopted a more cautious approach, focusing on defensive solidity over attacking ambition. Their limited entries into the final third and penalty area reflect a strategy of soaking up pressure and looking for counter-attacking opportunities. While they’ve managed to keep Leverkusen at bay so far, their lack of possession and attacking intent might prove costly if they cannot capitalize on the rare chances they create, as seen with Luca Waldschmidt’s missed opportunity.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: Throughout the first half, Leverkusen maintained a consistent attacking presence, with frequent spells of dominance in the opposition half, particularly in the early stages and towards the end of the half. Their momentum rarely dipped, showcasing their intent to control the game. In contrast, Köln had brief periods of attacking momentum, mainly around the middle of the half, where they showed glimpses of danger on the counter. However, these moments were short-lived, with Leverkusen quickly regaining control of the attacking flow.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Leverkusen are likely to continue their high-pressing game and exploit their dominance in the final third. If they can improve their finishing, they should be able to find the back of the net. Köln will need to remain compact defensively and look for quick transitions to catch Leverkusen off guard. A potential tactical shift or substitution could help Köln gain a foothold, but Leverkusen’s depth and home advantage at BayArena make them the favorites to take the lead.
